Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF NAVID SARRAFZADEH'S Motion To Lift Stay

Plaintiff's "motion to terminate stay" is denied.

These disputes spawned an arbitration and a litigation case (CGC-20-587022). In 2021, the parties stipulated that "resolution of the disputes currently pending in the arbitration may bear upon or relate to claims asserted in the instant litigation" and "the parties hereby agree that a stay of this litigation will avoid divergent outcomes and promote judicial economy." Thus, this court stayed the litigation "pending the final adjudication of all claims in the arbitration."

Plaintiff first moved to lift the stay in August 2023, but this court ruled that, as to the arbitration, "potential appeal remain[s], so the claims are not finally resolved." This is consistent with Code of Civil Procedure 1049's statement that an "action is deemed to be pending from the time of its commencement until its final determination upon appeal." Defendants say plaintiff "has made clear that he intends to appeal" the arbitration's results - a point plaintiff's papers do not dispute.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR#5229, 415-828-1916, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
